constraints_1_content: Before writing /app/cache_stats.py, use the Read tool to inspect /app/numbers.txt so you understand its exact line endings, spacing patterns, and edge cases before writing any parsing logic.
constraints_1_rationale: Directly tied to the specific input file in this task; uses ClaudeCode's Read tool.
constraints_2_content: Use Bash to run `redis-cli -h 127.0.0.1 -p 6379 PING` before writing any code, to confirm Redis connectivity early and decide whether a daemon start is needed.
constraints_2_rationale: Specific to Redis connectivity verification in this task; relies on Bash tool in ClaudeCode.
constraints_3_content: After running the script, verify correctness with Bash: check `cat /app/result.txt` and `redis-cli -h 127.0.0.1 -p 6379 GET stats:avg` and `redis-cli -h 127.0.0.1 -p 6379 TTL stats:avg` in a single verification step before declaring the task complete.
constraints_3_rationale: Uses ClaudeCode's Bash tool for end-to-end verification specific to the Redis key and file outputs of this task.
constraints_5_content: Define Redis connection parameters (host, port) as module-level constants or variables at the top of the script — do not scatter magic strings like `'127.0.0.1'` and `6379` throughout the code.
constraints_5_rationale: Specific to this Redis task's connection configuration; does not reference any ClaudeCode tool.
constraints_6_content: Add a `#!/usr/bin/env python3` shebang line as the first line of /app/cache_stats.py and ensure the file has execute permission (`chmod +x`) after creation, so it is directly runnable as a standalone script.
constraints_6_rationale: Script portability detail specific to this standalone Python script task; does not depend on ClaudeCode tools.
constraints_7_content: When reading /app/numbers.txt, use Python's built-in file I/O rather than Bash `cat` or `awk` pipelines. Reserve Bash tool usage for environment checks (Redis ping, package inspection, post-run verification) rather than core data processing.
constraints_7_rationale: Defines clear separation between Bash tool usage and Python logic specific to this data-processing task.
